question: A team is reviewing the Camunda Spring Boot Starter introduced in 8.8 before adopting it. Which of the following statements about it is **NOT** true?

options.text: It uses gRPC as its default protocol and requires the Spring Zeebe SDK as a runtime dependency.
The Spring Zeebe SDK is scheduled to be removed in Camunda 8.10, and teams should migrate before that upgrade.
It is part of the Camunda 8 public API and follows Semantic Versioning, except for alpha features.
It uses the new Camunda Java Client under the hood.

a.text: This statement is false on both counts: REST is the default protocol (gRPC is _configurable_), and the new starter _replaces_ the Spring Zeebe SDK rather than depending on it.

b.text: True — the docs state "Spring Zeebe SDK will be removed in version 8.10" and direct teams to migrate before that upgrade to avoid breaking changes.

c.text: True — the docs note the starter is part of the public API and follows SemVer except for alpha features; minor and patch releases will not introduce breaking changes.

d.text: True — the migration callout states the new starter "uses the new Camunda Java Client under the hood".

explanation: The Camunda Spring Boot Starter replaces the Spring Zeebe SDK as of 8.8. REST is the default protocol (gRPC is configurable), it is part of the public API with SemVer guarantees, and it wraps the new Camunda Java Client. The false statement combines two wrong claims: gRPC-by-default and a Spring Zeebe SDK runtime dependency.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - Getting started
